references.name: mindmap_display_layout
references.description: Mermaid mindmap uses radial auto-layout. Default depth 3 for all top-level groups. Architecture and constraints omitted in normal mode. Session has near_memory and far_memory at level 1; near_memory at depth 4 (shows category children); near_memory children are top-level group names (conversation, conventions, work, documentation) with recent activities under each category; always show all 4 categories even when empty (display '(none)'). Deep subtrees shrink the radial layout — keep balanced by controlling depth. Full mode: all nodes expanded at max depth. User can request specific branches at specific depths. Section 3: memory stats table (Store/Count/Size/Tokens/Loaded) showing disk vs context occupancy per store, with /context hint.

references.name: theme_default
references.description: Use mermaid default theme for mindmap rendering. Always include %%{init: {'theme': 'default', 'mindmap': {'useMaxWidth': true}}}%% directive in output. This provides automatic multi-color nodes per branch depth and forces full available width. Custom blue theme is not supported by mermaid mindmap type.

references.name: far_memory_topic_splitting
references.description: When far_memory.json grows large, Claude splits it into topic-based archive files. Each archive groups messages by summarized subject (from near_memory summaries). Main far_memory.json keeps only the active conversation topic. Archive files are stored in sessions/archives/ named by topic slug (e.g., far_memory_architecture_design.json). Main file maintains an 'archives' index array with {file, topic, message_range, near_memory_range}. On split, Claude identifies topic boundaries from near_memory summary clusters, extracts completed topics to archive files, and updates the index. mind-context default view shows the last active conversation from near_memory.

references.name: programs_over_improvisation
references.description: Claude-as-engine is bootstrap only (new session, resume, compaction recovery). All mechanical operations use Python scripts in scripts/. Claude provides intelligence (summaries, topic names) as arguments to deterministic programs. When architecture evolves, scripts must be updated to match — programs ARE the implementation of the architecture.

references.name: staging_paths
references.description: Two staging paths from work. Convention staging: when a pattern proves reusable, extract it to conventions.json as a self-contained template — conventions never reference far_memory, they must be reproducible standalone by any future session. Content staging: documents, code, artifacts stay linked to memory — work.json points to far_memory ranges so the full creation context can be recalled for continuation or revision.

references.name: node_text_rules
references.description: Mindmap node text must be human-readable descriptions only. No raw IDs, internal pointers, or inline refs in node labels. Avoid mermaid reserved words (e.g. 'mindmap', 'graph', 'flowchart') in node text as they cause syntax errors.

references.name: unified_chrome_bar
references.description: All panels (interface, center, right) use the same chrome bar convention built by buildChromeBar(). Non-collapsible part: 'Actual Page' permalink link to production. Collapsible part (toggle arrow, collapsed by default): full metadata block — pub_id, title with version badge, tagline/description, pub_meta, pub_date, source_url, generated timestamp, citation. Replaces old doc-header and embed-topbar. State persisted in localStorage per panel. Front matter fields: pub_id, pub_meta, pub_version, pub_date, header_title, tagline, source_url, citation, permalink, permalink_fr.

references.name: toc_two_columns
references.description: Table of contents uses CSS two-column layout when there are 8 or more heading entries. Uses CSS columns property with break-inside:avoid on list items. Threshold: 8+ entries triggers toc-twocol class.

references.name: interface_panel_routing
references.description: When viewer runs embedded in content-frame (right panel of main navigator): interface links route to center-frame instead of loading in-place, main navigator link reloads the full page. External links (different origin) always open in a new browser tab. Routing is handled in the viewer's interceptInternalLinks using window.name detection. The navigator's srcdoc iframe has null origin so cross-document access from navigator to panel content is not possible.

references.name: soft_coupled_baseurl
references.description: Viewer auto-detects SITE.baseurl from window.location.pathname at runtime. Production baseurl (/knowledge) is kept as fallback for resolving Liquid tags in imported markdown. resolveInternalPath and viewerRewriteUrl both check viewer baseurl first, then production baseurl. resolveLiquid handles both single and double-quoted Liquid syntax.

references.name: doc_header_convention
references.description: Doc-header bar displays: toggle arrow (left), nav-back link (for /full/ pages back to summary), actual page permalink, version dot+tag. Collapsible section shows labeled rows: Publication (version · date · title), Generated (timestamp), Authors (default: Martin Paquet & Claude). Built by shared buildDocHeaderParts(meta) used in both standalone doc-header and embed chrome bar. Authors split by & displayed on separate lines.

references.name: export_corporate_styling
references.description: PDF and DOCX exports use corporate styling, not web theme colors. White background (#fff), black text (#111), corporate blue accent (#1d4ed8). Cover page centered horizontally with title, description, separator line, pub_meta, timestamp, authors. TOC on page 2 with page-break-after. Running header: title with blue bottom liner. Running footer: Generated timestamp · version, K_DOCS right-aligned, blue top liner. First page (cover) has empty header/footer. Filename: PUB_ID - Title - VER.pdf/.docx.

references.name: draggable_panel_dividers
references.description: Main navigator vertical dividers support both drag (mousedown/mousemove/mouseup with overlay) and click-to-step fallback. Left divider cycles [0, 220, 320]px. Right divider cycles 0 → 50% → full → 0. Grid uses pixel-based grid-template-columns with transition disabled during drag. Dividers 14px wide on desktop, 8px on mobile (≤768px) via CSS media query + JS DIVIDER_W. Styled with 5 grip dots, no arrows.

references.name: display_conventions_and_stats
references.description: Display convention system for K_MIND mindmap. depth_config.json provides human-editable per-branch depth settings. set_depth.py manages depth config updates. mindmap_filter.py renders depth-filtered mindmap from config. memory_stats.py computes disk/token stats with context availability. /mind-stats and /mind-depth skills provide standalone access. Promoted from work to conventions after validation.
mind_memory_refs: knowledge::conventions::claude desktop app::display conventions and stats
